question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the enabled flag check in tracePostExecution() is separate from stateFilterCheck(), and by timing requests to coincide with a configuration reload that temporarily sets enabled=true with empty filters, cause a burst of unbounded logging that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user send a contract call that triggers a deep call chain where each frame has a different recipient address, and since contractFilterCheck() compares against a fixed set, all frames with addresses not in the set pass through and get logged, causing a log flood that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() is invoked synchronously within the EVM execution loop, and by crafting a contract that generates thousands of state transitions per second, cause the logging overhead to slow down EVM execution enough to cause the node to miss network heartbeats and partition from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the contract set uses java.util.HashSet which has O(1) average but O(n) worst-case lookup due to hash collisions, and by crafting contract addresses that all hash to the same bucket, cause contractFilterCheck() to degrade to O(n) and slow down the tracer enough to partition the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the status set is a HashSet<State> where State is an enum, and by sending concurrent requests that cause concurrent iteration and modification of the set during stateFilterCheck(), trigger a ConcurrentModificationException that crashes the tracer thread and causes a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() is called after commonEntityAccessor.get() which performs a database lookup, and by sending a high volume of requests, cause the combination of DB lookups and log emissions to saturate both DB connections and log I/O, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when the status set contains all possible State enum values, stateFilterCheck() returns false for all states (all pass through), and by sending a high volume of contract calls, cause every frame to be logged and partition the node from the network via log saturation?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() does not limit the size of the contract set, and if the set grows very large (e.g., via configuration injection), cause HashSet.contains() to consume significant memory and CPU, degrading node performance and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() is called on every EVM opcode execution (not just at frame boundaries), and by crafting a contract with maximum opcodes per gas unit, cause the tracer to be invoked millions of times per second, saturating the log system and partitioning the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() is invoked with a hex string derived from the entity ID, and by targeting contracts whose entity IDs produce hex strings that collide with filter entries in a case-sensitive comparison, bypass the filter and cause unbounded logging that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the TraceProperties bean is a singleton shared across all request threads, and by sending concurrent requests that cause concurrent reads of the status set in stateFilterCheck(), trigger a race condition that causes inconsistent filter behavior and unbounded logging that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() has no maximum execution time guard, and by crafting a scenario where the contract set is very large and the lookup is slow, cause the tracer to block the EVM execution thread long enough to miss network heartbeats and partition the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() returns false (log everything) when the status set is empty, and by sending a contract call that generates a very deep call stack (up to EVM call depth limit of 1024), cause 1024 log entries per request and partition the node from the network via log volume amplification?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() is called with the result of CommonUtils.hex(toEvmAddress(entity.getId())), and if the entity ID conversion produces an unexpected format, cause a mismatch that bypasses the filter and logs all frames, enabling a log flood that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the log.info() call in tracePostExecution() logs frame.getInputData().toShortHexString() which can be up to 131072 bytes, and when stateFilterCheck() returns false for all frames, cause each log entry to be extremely large and saturate the log aggregation network, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() is called after a database lookup (commonEntityAccessor.get()), and by sending a high volume of requests targeting contracts not in the DB, cause the combination of DB misses and unconditional logging (contractFilterCheck returns false when set is empty) to saturate both DB and log I/O, partitioning the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() does not account for the EXCEPTIONAL_HALT state being a terminal state, and by crafting contracts that always halt exceptionally, cause a flood of EXCEPTIONAL_HALT log entries that saturate the log system and partition the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() is invoked for every EVM operation frame (not just per-transaction), and by crafting a contract with a tight loop of cheap opcodes, cause contractFilterCheck() to be called millions of times per second, degrading node performance and causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the TraceProperties.enabled flag is checked before stateFilterCheck() in tracePostExecution(), and if the enabled flag is true but the status set is empty, cause stateFilterCheck() to always return false (log everything), enabling a log flood that partitions the node from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() uses a HashSet which does not preserve insertion order, and by crafting a scenario where the set is modified concurrently during a contains() check, cause a ConcurrentModificationException that disrupts the tracer and causes a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() is called synchronously in the hot path of EVM execution, and by sending a burst of concurrent contract calls that each generate many state transitions, cause the logging overhead to create a backpressure that slows down the EVM execution thread pool and causes the node to miss network messages, partitioning it from the network?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that contractFilterCheck() does not validate the length of the contract string parameter, and if a very long string is passed (e.g., due to a bug in hex encoding), cause the HashSet.contains() call to be slow and degrade node performance, causing a network partition?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the status set is initialized as a new HashSet<>() (empty by default), meaning stateFilterCheck() always returns false initially, and by sending requests before any configuration is applied, cause all frames to be logged and partition the node from the network via log flood?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: contractFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the contract set is initialized as a new HashSet<>() (empty by default), meaning contractFilterCheck() always returns false initially, and by sending requests before any configuration is applied, cause all contract frames to be logged and partition the node from the network via log flood?

[File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/evm/properties/TraceProperties.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: stateFilterCheck()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stateFilterCheck() is called with the result of frame.getState() which may return different values during concurrent execution, and by sending concurrent requests, cause a race condition where the state changes between the stateFilterCheck() call and the log.info() call, causing inconsistent logging behavior that floods the log system and partitions the node from the network?
